summary: Dependency-confusion package exfiltrates host, network, and environment data on install
details: Package is published at version 99.0.0 under an internal-sounding name (`tempo-shared-modules`) — the classic dependency-confusion shape that overrides a private registry resolution. Its `preinstall` script (poc.js) runs `ipconfig /all` on Windows or `ip a && cat /etc/resolv.conf` on Linux, collects `os.hostname()`, `os.platform()`, username, install directory, network interfaces, all environment variable names, and all `npm_*` environment variable values, then POSTs the JSON payload to `d8a56vpon5budaeafq00tsyj88aqd5m7p.oast.pro` (a Project Discovery interactsh out-of-band callback domain) and also performs a DNS callback to the same host. The package's own description identifies it as a Walmart HackerOne dependency-confusion PoC referencing `@livingdesign/react`, but the runtime behavior is genuine installer-side reconnaissance and data exfiltration: any environment that resolves this name from public npm instead of its private registry will execute the beacon and leak host, network, and npm-related secrets to an external endpoint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
